seoTitle: Flying Fox vs Freds Pass — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Flying Fox and Freds Pass. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Flying Fox vs Freds Pass: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Flying Fox and Freds Pass.
heading: Flying Fox vs Freds Pass — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Flying Fox, Northern Territory and Freds Pass, Northern Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Freds Pass has a lower overall climate risk score (27/100) compared to Flying Fox (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Flying Fox or Freds Pass safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Flying Fox has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Freds Pass scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Flying Fox scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Freds Pass scores 34/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Flying Fox or Freds Pass?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Freds Pass has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.47% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
